rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by using only visible identifiers from the current component scope.
Ensure type consistency in AGREE expressions by matching left and right sides of assignments.
Move implementation-level bindings to the correct implementation scope and avoid type errors from invalid references.
Delete invalid AGREE clauses that reference unresolved identifiers instead of adding new architecture.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must reference only visible identifiers and declared helpers within the same annex scope.
Type mismatches in AGREE expressions should be resolved by aligning helper declarations with their assigned expressions.
Component type annexes should not contain implementation-level references or subcomponent paths.
Validator diagnostics pointing to unresolved identifiers should be addressed by removing or rewriting clauses that use them.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use only declared constants, equations, and visible ports in AGREE expressions.
Ensure numeric, boolean, and real types are correctly assigned in AGREE declarations.
Implementation-level assignments must reside in component implementation scopes.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the model.
Do not attempt to define new identifiers or components in AGREE contracts during repair.
Do not assume that missing references can be resolved by adding new architecture elements.
Avoid generating AGREE expressions with mismatched types or unresolved helper functions.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE annex placement in component types vs. implementations
Model Analyst agent's structural extraction tasks
